a.xlink:title: Initializes the acceleration.
Performs one acceleration step.

: computes number of cols in least squares system, i.e, number of cols in _matrixV, _matrixW, _qrV, etc.. This is necessary only for master-slave mode, when some procs do not have any nodes on the coupling interface. In this case, the matrices are not constructed and we have no information about the number of cols. This info is needed for master-slave communication. Number of its =! _cols in general.
Marks a iteration sequence as converged.
how many QN columns were deleted in this timestep
how many QN columns were dropped (went out of scope) in this timestep
Initializes the acceleration.
Returns true, if given element is in vector, otherwise false.
Checks if all dataIDs are contained in cplData.
True if this process is running the master.
True if this process is running a slave.
Number of ranks. This includes ranks from both participants, e.g. minimal size is 2...
Current rank.
Concatenates all coupling data involved in the QN system in a single vector.
Updates the V, W matrices (as well as the matrices for the secondary data)
The l2 norm of a vector is calculated on distributed data.
Computes underrelaxation for the secondary data.
resets the QR factorization zo zero Q(0:0, 0:0)R(0:0, 0:0)
Applies the filter method for the least-squares system, defined in the configuration.
Computes the quasi-Newton update using the specified pp scheme (MVQN, IQNILS)
Splits up QN system vector back into the coupling data.
Compares two Eigen::MatrixBase for equality up to tolerance.

updates the factorization A=Q[1:n,1:m]R[1:m,1:n] when the kth column of A is deleted. Returns the deleted column v(1:n)

Removes one iteration from V,W matrices and adapts _matrixCols.
Marks a iteration sequence as converged.
a very similar test, but with a vertex that has been completely filtered out
